SAN JOSE NURSING CENTER

CCN: 45E312 · SAN ANTONIO, TX 78221 · Bexar County

OverviewRatingsInspectionsDeficienciesOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
406 SHARMAIN PL, SAN ANTONIO, TX 78221
Phone
2109248136
Certified beds
55
Avg daily residents
34 (62% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1974-03-31
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Resident count too small for reliable rating.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-04-11 15 15 1 76 1 76
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-03-08 12 5 7 52 1 52
Total weighted health score
70.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
4
last cycle

Deficiencies (32)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

3 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
29 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. E 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2025-04-11 2025-05-16
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2025-01-30 2025-03-05
0729 Verify that a nurse aide has been trained; and if they haven't worked as a nurse aide for 2 years, receive retraining. D 2025-01-30 2025-03-05
0943 Give their staff education on dementia care, and what abuse, neglect, and exploitation are; and how to report abuse, neglect, and exploitation. D 2025-01-30 2025-03-05
0639 Maintain 15 months of resident assessments in the resident's active clinical record. E 2024-03-08 2024-04-05
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-03-08 2024-04-05
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-03-08 2024-04-05
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2024-03-08 2024-04-05
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2024-03-08 2024-04-05
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2023-12-20 2024-01-22
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2023-12-20 2024-01-22
0626 Permit a resident to return to the nursing home after hospitalization or therapeutic leave that exceeds bed-hold policy. D 2023-12-20 2024-01-22
0729 Verify that a nurse aide has been trained; and if they haven't worked as a nurse aide for 2 years, receive retraining. D 2023-12-20 2024-01-22
0638 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. D 2023-02-03 2023-03-13
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2023-02-03 2023-03-13
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2023-02-03 2023-03-13
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-02-03 2023-03-13
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2023-02-03 —
